pages.text: All Magic Storage blocks must connect by $(thing)direct adjacency$() - they must share a face (up, down, north, south, east, or west).$(br2)Cables or conduits are $(thing)not needed$(). Simply place blocks next to each other.

When you place a new network block, the Core attempts a $(thing)small incremental add$() for that block. When a block is broken or the topology is uncertain, the Core falls back to a full $(thing)BFS (Breadth-First Search)$() rebuild from the Core. The safety scan is capped to avoid runaway searches.$(br2)If the network touches more than one Core, the network becomes $(thing)conflicted$() and stops accepting items until the extra Core is removed.

$(thing)Pro tips:$()$(br2)$(li)You can build in any shape you like - lines, cubes, or sprawling webs.$(li)All network blocks (Units, Terminals, Buses) must be in loaded chunks for reliable operation.$(li)Breaking the Storage Core detaches the network; replacing a Core at the same position during the same server session restores its saved Core data. Do not rely on moving the Core elsewhere.
